title: New Chaos Variant Targets Misconfigured Cloud Deployments, Adds SOCKS Proxy
summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new variant ofmalware called Chaosthat'scapable of hitting misconfigured cloud deployments, marking an expansion of the botnet's targeting infrastructure.

summary: When  means "bot available,"  signifies "toolkit," or  translates to "big ransom," bad actors can evade filters and keep it all on the down-low.

title: AI-Led Remediation Crisis Prompts HackerOne to Pause Bug Bounties
summary: Discovery used to be the bottleneck for open source bugs, but with automated discovery, remediation's the bottleneck, which bounties don't fund.

title: APT28 Deploys PRISMEX Malware in Campaign Targeting Ukraine and NATO Allies
summary: The Russian threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented malware suite codenamed PRISMEX.

sections.context: This reflects continued use of phishing as an initial-access path in broader intrusion chains.

email_summary: The Russian threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented malware suite codenamed PRISMEX. Severity is rated high.

title: Fraud Rockets Higher in Mobile-First Latin America
summary: Cyber-fraudsters move quickly from compromised devices to account takeover to funds transfer, shifting money before many financial institutions can react.

sections.context: This fits the broader data-exposure trend where follow-on abuse of leaked information is common.

title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Finds Thousands of Zero-Day Flaws Across Major Systems
summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) company Anthropic announced a new cybersecurity initiative called Project Glasswing that will use a preview version of its new frontier model, Claude Mythos, to find and address security vulnerabilities.

sections.context: This story is part of the recurring zero-day pattern where exploitation pressure is highest before broad patch adoption.

title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt U.S. Critical Infrastructure by Targeting Internet-Exposed PLCs
summary: Iran-affiliated cyber actors are targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices across critical infrastructures in the U.S., including programmable logic controllers (PLCs), cybersecurity and intelligence agencies warned Tuesday.
